activation-drift-monitor.summary: Released reusable package `activation-drift-monitor` (v0.1.0) as a Python-first library with extracted core drift-monitoring APIs, companion stdio MCP interface (`python -m activation_drift_monitor.mcp_server`), machine-readable packaging manifest/registry metadata, and built wheel+sdist artifacts. Canonical install flow: `pip install omegaxiv` then `ox install activation-drift-monitor==0.1.0` (optional additive MCP registration via `--mcp`, `--mcp=codex`, `--mcp=claude`, or `--mcp=all`).

antineutrino-safeguards.summary: Released antineutrino-safeguards v0.1.0 as a reusable Python library (`antineutrino_safeguards`) extracted from EXP-ED-01 core simulation, claim-summary, and symbolic-audit symbols. Canonical user flow is `pip install omegaxiv` then `ox install antineutrino-safeguards==0.1.0`; additive MCP registration is `ox install antineutrino-safeguards==0.1.0 --mcp` (or `--mcp=codex|claude|all`). Standard imports use `FusionSafeguardsSimulator`, `MonitoringConfig`, and `summarize_claims`. MCP companion interface launches via stdio with `python -m antineutrino_safeguards.mcp_server --serve` and exposes `simulate_monitoring`, `summarize_claims`, `rank_regimes`, and `symbolic_audit` tools.

ccgo-rl.summary: Packaging retry completed for handle ccgo-rl (library, v0.1.0). Canonical user install flow remains `pip install omegaxiv` then `ox install ccgo-rl==0.1.0`; MCP remains additive via `ox install ccgo-rl==0.1.0 --mcp` (or `--mcp=codex|claude|all`). Core exports now map to importable packaged symbols (`ccgo_rl.simulator.simulate_benchmark_rows`, `simulate_counterexamples`, `summarize_by_baseline`, and `ccgo_rl.symbolic.run_checks`) extracted from project contribution sources. Built artifacts were rebuilt and checksummed, and installed-artifact smoke validation passed for both Python API import/use and `python -m ccgo_rl.mcp_server --help` from a clean environment.

cl-act.summary: Reusable package artifacts for activation_cl_validation are staged under workspace/packages/cl-act with validated lint/type/test and clean wheel install/import smoke evidence; release finalization remains blocked because upstream review_feedback decision is iterate and force_packaging_release was not provided.

fft-policy-selector.summary: Reusable library release for scenario-conditioned Fourier policy selection. Install with `pip install omegaxiv` then `ox install fft-policy-selector==0.1.0`. For Python usage, import from `fft_policy_selector`, for example `from fft_policy_selector import ScenarioConditionedSelector, selector_policy`.

lottery-bias-kit.summary: Packaged approved outputs as reusable library `omegaxiv-lottery-bias-kit` (`lottery_bias_kit` import path) with extracted core implementations, tests, examples, manifest, dependency graph, registry metadata, and built artifacts. Canonical user install flow is `pip install omegaxiv` then `ox install lottery-bias-kit==0.1.0`; maintainer/dev install target is `packages/lottery-bias-kit/dist/omegaxiv_lottery_bias_kit-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl`. Import guidance: `from lottery_bias_kit import LotteryBiasAnalyzer`.

qrc-hybrid-core.summary: Packaged as a reusable Python library exposing project-core stage logic (parity advantage, entanglement phase diagnostics, operator attribution) under import path `qrchybrid`. Canonical user install is `pip install omegaxiv` then `ox install qrc-hybrid-core==0.1.0`. Primary imports: `from qrchybrid import summarize_parity_advantage, evaluate_entanglement_phase_map, evaluate_operator_attribution`.

qrc-ising-core.summary: Released and revalidated qrc-ising-core 0.1.0 as a reusable Python library with additive MCP stdio companion interface. Canonical user flow remains: (1) pip install omegaxiv, (2) ox install qrc-ising-core==0.1.0, with optional --mcp registration (--mcp, --mcp=codex, --mcp=claude, --mcp=all).

yb171-raman-gate.summary: Release package yb171-raman-gate v0.1.0 is finalized as a reusable Python library extracted from UP1 core contribution symbols. Canonical user flow: pip install omegaxiv, then ox install yb171-raman-gate==0.1.0. Import guidance: from yb171_raman_gate import RamanGateModel, evaluate_gate_point, build_tradeoff_table, fit_surrogate.
